steps.title: Fastest path: identify the circle data first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, identify the center and radius from the wording or equation first. Then substitute those values into the standard circle equation.

steps.title: Use the circle equation form
steps.content: A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation $$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2.$$

steps.title: Substitute the center and radius
steps.content: The center is $(14,19)$ and the radius is $8k$, so $$(x-14)^2+(y-19)^2=(8k)^2.$$
